title: Identify the Stressor
description: Notice what situation triggers your stressful thought.
steps: Write down the specific situation.
Notice how your body feels.
Write the literal thought as if it was a quote.

title: Spotting All-or-Nothing
description: Find shades of grey in your thinking.
steps: Write your absolute thought (e.g. ruined, always, never).
List one exception to this rule.
Rewrite the thought to include 'sometimes' or 'partially'.

title: 5-4-3-2-1 Technique
description: Use your senses to anchor yourself in the present.
steps: Acknowledge 5 things you see.
Acknowledge 4 things you can touch.
Acknowledge 3 things you hear.
Acknowledge 2 things you smell.
Acknowledge 1 thing you can taste.

title: Box Breathing
description: Calm your nervous system with measured breaths.
steps: Breathe in for 4 seconds.
Hold for 4 seconds.
Exhale for 4 seconds.
Hold empty for 4 seconds.
Repeat 5 times.

title: Three Good Things
description: Shift focus to positives from today.
steps: Write down one good thing.
Write down a second good thing.
Write down a third good thing.
Reflect on how these make you feel.

title: Inner Friend
description: Talk to yourself as you would a friend.
steps: Write down your self-critical thought.
Imagine a dear friend having this thought.
Write down what you would tell them.
Read those words back to yourself.

title: Do One Small Thing
description: Break the cycle of avoidance.
steps: Pick a task that takes under 5 minutes.
Set a timer for 5 minutes.
Do the task immediately.
Check in on your mood afterwards.

title: Imaginal Exposure
description: Visualize a mildly anxiety-provoking scenario.
steps: Sit somewhere safe.
Close your eyes and imagine the scenario.
Rate your anxiety 1-10.
Breathe until it drops by 2 points.

title: Challenge the Thought
description: Examine a recurring negative belief.
steps: Identify the negative belief.
Ask: Is it 100% true?
Ask: What would I tell a friend?
Reframe the thought.

title: Mind Reading Check
description: Stop assuming you know what others think.
steps: Notice who you are mind-reading.
List alternative reasons for their behavior.
Remind yourself you cannot read minds.

title: Color Focus
description: Ground yourself visually by finding colors.
steps: Pick a color.
Find 5 objects of that color in the room.
Describe each object out loud.
Pick another color and repeat.

title: Progressive Relaxation
description: Release tension from your muscles.
steps: Tense your toes for 5 seconds.
Release and notice the relaxation.
Move up to your calves, tense and release.
Continue moving up your body to your face.

title: Appreciation Letter
description: Express gratitude mentally or in writing.
steps: Think of someone who helped you.
Write a short paragraph of what they did.
Notice any warm feelings in your chest.

title: Forgiveness Practice
description: Let go of a small mistake.
steps: Acknowledge the mistake without judgment.
Remind yourself everyone makes mistakes.
Take a deep breath and visualize letting it go.

title: Pleasant Activity Scheduling
description: Plan a small enjoyment.
steps: Brainstorm 3 simple things you enjoy.
Pick one to do today.
Schedule an exact time.
Do it.

title: Worry Time
description: Confine worry to a specific block of time.
steps: Schedule 15 minutes of 'worry time'.
During the day, jot down worries and postpone them.
When the time comes, review the list.
When time is up, stop and do another activity.

title: Examine the Evidence
description: Look at facts objectively.
steps: Write down the belief.
List evidence that supports it.
List evidence against it.
Incorporate both into a balanced thought.

title: Label Reducer
description: Change global labels to specific behaviors.
steps: Catch yourself calling yourself a name.
Identify the specific behavior you regret.
Acknowledge that one behavior doesn't define you.

title: Texture Walk
description: Focus on the physical sensation of touch.
steps: Find an object near you.
Feel its texture with your fingertips.
Describe the texture (smooth, rough, cold).
Find a second object and compare.

title: Shoulder Drop
description: Release physical tension held in your shoulders.
steps: Notice if your shoulders are raised.
Take a deep breath in.
Exhale forcibly while dropping shoulders.
Roll them back twice.

title: Silver Linings
description: Find one positive in a neutral or negative situation.
steps: Pick a minor annoyance from today.
Find one small silver lining in it.
Focus on that silver lining for 30 seconds.

title: Soothing Touch
description: Provide yourself with physical comfort.
steps: Place a hand over your heart or on your arm.
Feel the warmth of your hand.
Take three deep, slow breaths.

title: Routine Anchor
description: Attach a new habit to an existing one.
steps: Identify something you do every day (like brushing teeth).
Choose a 2-minute positive action.
Do them together.

title: Decatastrophize a Fear
description: Examine the feared outcome.
steps: Write down what you fear will happen.
Write down how you would survive it.
Write down a more realistic outcome.

title: De-catastrophizing
description: Manage worst-case scenario thinking.
steps: Identify the worst-case scenario.
Estimate how likely it really is.
Imagine how you would cope if it happened.
Identify the best-case scenario.

title: Should Statements
description: Replace 'should' with 'would like'.
steps: Write down a 'should' statement.
Change it to 'I would prefer' or 'It would be nice if'.
Notice how the pressure changes.

title: Temperature Notice
description: Use temperature to ground your body.
steps: Hold an ice cube or something cold.
Focus entirely on the cold sensation.
Notice how your hand feels as it warns up.

title: 4-7-8 Breathing
description: A relaxing breath pattern.
steps: Inhale quietly through your nose for 4 seconds.
Hold the breath for 7 seconds.
Exhale completely through your mouth for 8 seconds.
Repeat 4 times.

title: Future Gratitude
description: Look forward to something positive.
steps: Think of one thing happening tomorrow to enjoy.
Visualize it.
Write down why you are looking forward to it.

title: Shared Humanity
description: Remember you are not alone.
steps: Notice a feeling of inadequacy.
Remind yourself that thousands feel this right now.
Acknowledge that struggling is part of being human.

title: Mastery Task
description: Do something that gives a sense of accomplishment.
steps: Pick a task you've been putting off.
Break it into an incredibly small first step.
Complete just that first step.

title: Safety Behavior Drop
description: Gradually remove crutches.
steps: Identify something you do just to feel 'safe'.
Try doing the activity without it once.
Notice that you survived without the safety behavior.

title: Identify the Stressor 2

steps: Write down the specific situation.
Notice how your body feels.
Write the literal thought as if it was a quote.

description: Let go of a small mistake.
steps: Acknowledge the mistake without judgment.
Remind yourself everyone makes mistakes.
Take a deep breath and visualize letting it go.

title: Pleasant Activity Scheduling 5
description: Plan a small enjoyment.
steps: Brainstorm 3 simple things you enjoy.
Pick one to do today.
Schedule an exact time.
Do it.

title: Worry Time 5
description: Confine worry to a specific block of time.
steps: Schedule 15 minutes of 'worry time'.
During the day, jot down worries and postpone them.
When the time comes, review the list.
When time is up, stop and do another activity.

title: Examine the Evidence 5
description: Look at facts objectively.
steps: Write down the belief.
List evidence that supports it.
List evidence against it.
Incorporate both into a balanced thought.

title: Label Reducer 5
description: Change global labels to specific behaviors.
steps: Catch yourself calling yourself a name.
Identify the specific behavior you regret.
Acknowledge that one behavior doesn't define you.

title: Texture Walk 5
description: Focus on the physical sensation of touch.
steps: Find an object near you.
Feel its texture with your fingertips.
Describe the texture (smooth, rough, cold).
Find a second object and compare.

title: Shoulder Drop 5
description: Release physical tension held in your shoulders.
steps: Notice if your shoulders are raised.
Take a deep breath in.
Exhale forcibly while dropping shoulders.
Roll them back twice.

title: Silver Linings 5
description: Find one positive in a neutral or negative situation.
steps: Pick a minor annoyance from today.
Find one small silver lining in it.
Focus on that silver lining for 30 seconds.

title: Soothing Touch 5
description: Provide yourself with physical comfort.
steps: Place a hand over your heart or on your arm.
Feel the warmth of your hand.
Take three deep, slow breaths.

title: Routine Anchor 5
description: Attach a new habit to an existing one.
steps: Identify something you do every day (like brushing teeth).
Choose a 2-minute positive action.
Do them together.

title: Decatastrophize a Fear 5
description: Examine the feared outcome.
steps: Write down what you fear will happen.
Write down how you would survive it.
Write down a more realistic outcome.

title: De-catastrophizing 5
description: Manage worst-case scenario thinking.
steps: Identify the worst-case scenario.
Estimate how likely it really is.
Imagine how you would cope if it happened.
Identify the best-case scenario.

title: Should Statements 5
description: Replace 'should' with 'would like'.
steps: Write down a 'should' statement.
Change it to 'I would prefer' or 'It would be nice if'.
Notice how the pressure changes.

title: Temperature Notice 5
description: Use temperature to ground your body.
steps: Hold an ice cube or something cold.
Focus entirely on the cold sensation.
Notice how your hand feels as it warns up.

title: 4-7-8 Breathing 5
description: A relaxing breath pattern.
steps: Inhale quietly through your nose for 4 seconds.
Hold the breath for 7 seconds.
Exhale completely through your mouth for 8 seconds.
Repeat 4 times.

title: Future Gratitude 5
description: Look forward to something positive.
steps: Think of one thing happening tomorrow to enjoy.
Visualize it.
Write down why you are looking forward to it.

title: Shared Humanity 5
description: Remember you are not alone.
steps: Notice a feeling of inadequacy.
Remind yourself that thousands feel this right now.
Acknowledge that struggling is part of being human.

title: Mastery Task 5
description: Do something that gives a sense of accomplishment.
steps: Pick a task you've been putting off.
Break it into an incredibly small first step.
Complete just that first step.

title: Safety Behavior Drop 5
description: Gradually remove crutches.
steps: Identify something you do just to feel 'safe'.
Try doing the activity without it once.
Notice that you survived without the safety behavior.

title: Identify the Stressor 6
description: Notice what situation triggers your stressful thought.
steps: Write down the specific situation.
Notice how your body feels.
Write the literal thought as if it was a quote.

title: Spotting All-or-Nothing 6
description: Find shades of grey in your thinking.
steps: Write your absolute thought (e.g. ruined, always, never).
List one exception to this rule.
Rewrite the thought to include 'sometimes' or 'partially'.

title: 5-4-3-2-1 Technique 6
description: Use your senses to anchor yourself in the present.
steps: Acknowledge 5 things you see.
Acknowledge 4 things you can touch.
Acknowledge 3 things you hear.
Acknowledge 2 things you smell.
Acknowledge 1 thing you can taste.

title: Box Breathing 6
description: Calm your nervous system with measured breaths.
steps: Breathe in for 4 seconds.
Hold for 4 seconds.
Exhale for 4 seconds.
Hold empty for 4 seconds.
Repeat 5 times.

title: Three Good Things 6
description: Shift focus to positives from today.
steps: Write down one good thing.
Write down a second good thing.
Write down a third good thing.
Reflect on how these make you feel.

title: Inner Friend 6
description: Talk to yourself as you would a friend.
steps: Write down your self-critical thought.
Imagine a dear friend having this thought.
Write down what you would tell them.
Read those words back to yourself.

title: Do One Small Thing 6
description: Break the cycle of avoidance.
steps: Pick a task that takes under 5 minutes.
Set a timer for 5 minutes.
Do the task immediately.
Check in on your mood afterwards.

title: Imaginal Exposure 6
description: Visualize a mildly anxiety-provoking scenario.
steps: Sit somewhere safe.
Close your eyes and imagine the scenario.
Rate your anxiety 1-10.
Breathe until it drops by 2 points.

title: Challenge the Thought 6
description: Examine a recurring negative belief.
steps: Identify the negative belief.
Ask: Is it 100% true?
Ask: What would I tell a friend?
Reframe the thought.

title: Mind Reading Check 6
description: Stop assuming you know what others think.
steps: Notice who you are mind-reading.
List alternative reasons for their behavior.
Remind yourself you cannot read minds.

title: Color Focus 6
description: Ground yourself visually by finding colors.
steps: Pick a color.
Find 5 objects of that color in the room.
Describe each object out loud.
Pick another color and repeat.

title: Progressive Relaxation 6
description: Release tension from your muscles.
steps: Tense your toes for 5 seconds.
Release and notice the relaxation.
Move up to your calves, tense and release.
Continue moving up your body to your face.

title: Appreciation Letter 6
description: Express gratitude mentally or in writing.
steps: Think of someone who helped you.
Write a short paragraph of what they did.
Notice any warm feelings in your chest.

title: Forgiveness Practice 6
description: Let go of a small mistake.
steps: Acknowledge the mistake without judgment.
Remind yourself everyone makes mistakes.
Take a deep breath and visualize letting it go.

title: Pleasant Activity Scheduling 6
description: Plan a small enjoyment.
steps: Brainstorm 3 simple things you enjoy.
Pick one to do today.
Schedule an exact time.
Do it.

When the time comes, review the list.
